test_cases.description: Open with an empty string path returns a descriptive error without performing any filesystem access or opening a database connection.
preconditions: No filesystem state exists for an empty path
internal/db package is compiled
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error with message "db: path must not be empty"; returned *DB is nil; no directories or files are created
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(""); assert(db == nil); assert(err != nil); assert(err.Error() == "db: path must not be empty")

test_cases.description: Open with a path that points to an existing directory returns a descriptive error containing the path, without opening any database connection.
preconditions: A directory exists at the target path (e.g. created via os.MkdirTemp)
internal/db package is compiled
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error containing the path string and 'is a directory, not a file'; returned *DB is nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: dir := t.TempDir(); db, err := Open(dir); assert(db == nil); assert(err != nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), dir)); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), "is a directory, not a file"))

test_cases.description: Input validation checks occur before any directory creation or database/sql.Open call, ensuring no partial filesystem state is produced for invalid inputs.
preconditions: A path to a non-existent parent directory is constructed
internal/db package is compiled
test_cases.expected: Open with empty string returns error immediately; no directories are created; no SQLite connection is attempted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: path := ""; db, err := Open(path); assert(err != nil); assert(db == nil); assert(no filesystem entry was created at path)

test_cases.description: Open creates the parent directory recursively when it does not exist before opening the SQLite connection.
preconditions: Temporary base directory exists from t.TempDir()
Parent subdirectory of the database path does not exist
test_cases.expected: Open succeeds; the directory <tempdir>/newsubdir is created; database file exists at the given path
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: base := t.TempDir(); path := filepath.Join(base, "newsubdir", "apikit.db"); db, err := Open(path); assert(err == nil); assert(db != nil); assert(directoryExists(filepath.Dir(path))); db.Close()

test_cases.description: The directory created by Open has mode 0700 (owner read/write/execute only) on non-Windows platforms.
preconditions: Temporary base directory from t.TempDir()
Test runs on a non-Windows platform (runtime.GOOS != "windows")
Parent directory of the database path does not exist
test_cases.expected: Directory created by Open has permission bits 0700; mode is hardcoded and not configurable
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: if runtime.GOOS == "windows" { t.Skip() }; base := t.TempDir(); path := filepath.Join(base, "sensitivesubdir", "apikit.db"); db, err := Open(path); assert(err == nil); info := os.Stat(filepath.Dir(path)); assert(info.Mode().Perm() == 0700); db.Close()

test_cases.description: Open skips directory creation and proceeds directly to the database connection when the parent directory already exists.
preconditions: Temporary directory created by t.TempDir() exists
No database file yet exists inside it
test_cases.expected: Open succeeds without error; parent directory is unchanged; database file is created
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: base := t.TempDir(); path := filepath.Join(base, "apikit.db"); db, err := Open(path); assert(err == nil); assert(db != nil); db.Close()

test_cases.description: Both Open and OpenMemory delegate all post-connection initialization to the same shared private initDB function; there is no duplicated initialization logic.
preconditions: internal/db source code is available for inspection
Both Open and OpenMemory paths are exercised
test_cases.expected: Both constructors produce a *DB with identical initialization (pool settings, foreign key enforcement, schema); static code inspection confirms a single initDB call site in each constructor
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db1, err1 := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "a.db")); assert(err1 == nil); db2, err2 := OpenMemory(); assert(err2 == nil); verifyTablesExist(db1.SqlDB); verifyTablesExist(db2.SqlDB); db1.Close(); db2.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB sets SetMaxOpenConns(1) and SetMaxIdleConns(1) on the *sql.DB handle immediately after connection, before executing any PRAGMAs.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory
test_cases.expected: After Open, db.SqlDB reports MaxOpenConnections = 1; connection pool enforces the single-connection constraint
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "b.db")); assert(err == nil); stats := db.SqlDB.Stats(); assert(stats.MaxOpenConnections == 1); db.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB executes PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL as a query when called from Open, reads the returned mode string, and validates it equals 'wal'.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory on a WAL-supporting filesystem
test_cases.expected: Open succeeds; querying PRAGMA journal_mode on the returned database returns 'wal'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "c.db")); assert(err == nil); row := db.SqlDB.QueryRow("PRAGMA journal_mode"); var mode string; row.Scan(&mode); assert(mode == "wal"); db.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB skips the WAL mode PRAGMA and validation when called from OpenMemory; all other initialization steps still apply.
preconditions: No filesystem state required
test_cases.expected: OpenMemory succeeds; all six tables exist; foreign keys are enforced; pool is configured; no error about WAL mode is returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := OpenMemory(); assert(err == nil); assert(db != nil); verifyTablesExist(db.SqlDB); verifyForeignKeysOn(db.SqlDB); db.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB executes PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON via db.Exec after WAL mode setup and before schema creation.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory
test_cases.expected: After Open, querying 'PRAGMA foreign_keys' returns 1 (enabled)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "d.db")); assert(err == nil); var fk int; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("PRAGMA foreign_keys").Scan(&fk); assert(fk == 1); db.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB executes all six CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements inside a single DEFERRED transaction in the correct dependency order.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory
test_cases.expected: All six tables (users, api_keys, pats, orgs, org_members, admin_config) exist after Open; table creation succeeds atomically
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "e.db")); assert(err == nil); for each table in ["users","api_keys","pats","orgs","org_members","admin_config"]: row := db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?", table); assert(row.Scan(&name) == nil); db.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB returns nil on success so the calling constructor can return a fully initialized *DB handle with no partial state possible.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory
test_cases.expected: Open returns (*DB, nil); the returned *DB is non-nil and fully usable for queries
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "f.db")); assert(err == nil); assert(db != nil); assert(db.SqlDB != nil); db.Close()

test_cases.description: Open executes PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL using QueryRow (not Exec) so the returned journal mode value can be read and validated.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path on a WAL-supporting filesystem
test_cases.expected: Open succeeds; subsequent PRAGMA journal_mode query on the open DB returns 'wal'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "g.db")); assert(err == nil); var mode string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("PRAGMA journal_mode").Scan(&mode); assert(mode == "wal"); db.Close()

test_cases.description: Open returns a formatted error containing the unexpected mode string when PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL returns a mode other than 'wal'.
preconditions: Test environment can simulate WAL mode failure (e.g. via mock or logic path inspection)
internal/db package is compiled
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error with message containing 'failed to enable WAL mode' and the returned mode string; *DB is nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: // This is verified indirectly: inspect that the error format matches when mode != "wal"; err := fmt.Errorf("db: failed to enable WAL mode: journal_mode is %q", "delete"); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), "failed to enable WAL mode")); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), "delete"))

test_cases.description: OpenMemory skips PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL and WAL mode validation because SQLite in-memory databases always return 'memory' as their journal mode.
preconditions: No filesystem state required
test_cases.expected: OpenMemory succeeds without error; no WAL error is returned despite in-memory database returning 'memory' as journal mode
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := OpenMemory(); assert(err == nil); assert(db != nil); var mode string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("PRAGMA journal_mode").Scan(&mode); assert(mode == "memory"); db.Close()

test_cases.description: Open wraps the driver error with the file path when the database file is corrupt, so operators can identify the problematic file.
preconditions: A file containing arbitrary non-SQLite bytes exists at the target path
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error containing the path string; the original driver error is wrapped via %w; *DB is nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "corrupt.db"); os.WriteFile(path, []byte("not a sqlite db"), 0644); db, err := Open(path); assert(db == nil); assert(err != nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), path)); assert(errors.Unwrap(err) != nil)

test_cases.description: Open applies the file-path error wrapper only to driver-level errors after database/sql.Open succeeds; input validation and directory creation errors are returned as-is.
preconditions: A corrupt file at the target path to trigger a driver error
A separate empty path to trigger an input validation error
test_cases.expected: Empty path error message does not contain 'failed to open database at'; corrupt file error message contains 'failed to open database at' and the path
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: _, emptyErr := Open(""); assert(!strings.Contains(emptyErr.Error(), "failed to open database at")); corruptPath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "c.db"); os.WriteFile(corruptPath, []byte("garbage"), 0644); _, corruptErr := Open(corruptPath); assert(strings.Contains(corruptErr.Error(), "failed to open database at")); assert(strings.Contains(corruptErr.Error(), corruptPath))

test_cases.description: The users table is created with the normative DDL including all required columns, constraints, and the composite UNIQUE on (provider, provider_id).
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via Open or OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: Table 'users' exists; columns id, username, email, full_name, role, status, provider, provider_id, created_at, updated_at are present; UNIQUE constraint on (provider, provider_id) is enforced
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); row := db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='users'"); var ddl string; row.Scan(&ddl); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "provider_id")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "UNIQUE")); _, err := db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO users VALUES ('id1','u1','e@e.com',NULL,'user','active','github','gh1','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); assert(err == nil); _, dupErr := db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO users VALUES ('id2','u2','e2@e.com',NULL,'user','active','github','gh1','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); assert(dupErr != nil)

test_cases.description: The api_keys table is created with the normative DDL including FK reference to users(id).
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory with foreign keys enabled
test_cases.expected: Table 'api_keys' exists with columns key_id, user_id, secret_hash, expires_days, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at; FK to users(id) is present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); row := db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='api_keys'"); var ddl string; row.Scan(&ddl); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "REFERENCES users(id)")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "secret_hash"))

test_cases.description: The pats table is created with the normative DDL including the permissions column and FK to users(id).
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: Table 'pats' exists with columns token_id, user_id, name, secret_hash, permissions, expires_days, expires_at, revoked_at, created_at; FK to users(id) is present
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); var ddl string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='pats'").Scan(&ddl); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "permissions")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "REFERENCES users(id)"))

test_cases.description: The orgs table is created with the normative DDL including UNIQUE constraints on name and slug.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: Table 'orgs' exists with columns id, name, slug, url, status, created_at, updated_at; UNIQUE constraints on name and slug are enforced
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); var ddl string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='orgs'").Scan(&ddl); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "slug")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "UNIQUE"))

test_cases.description: The org_members table is created with composite PK (org_id, user_id), FK to orgs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, and FK to users(id).
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory with users and orgs rows for FK testing
test_cases.expected: Table 'org_members' exists; ON DELETE CASCADE on org_id causes membership rows to be deleted when the referenced org is deleted
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); var ddl string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='org_members'").Scan(&ddl); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "ON DELETE CASCADE")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "PRIMARY KEY (org_id, user_id)"))

test_cases.description: The admin_config table is created with the normative DDL: key TEXT NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY, value TEXT NOT NULL.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: Table 'admin_config' exists with columns key and value; key is the primary key
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); var ddl string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='admin_config'").Scan(&ddl); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "key")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "value")); assert(strings.Contains(ddl, "PRIMARY KEY"))

test_cases.description: All six CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements are executed inside a single DEFERRED transaction that commits atomically.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory
test_cases.expected: After Open, all six tables exist; the schema transaction was committed atomically
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "h.db")); assert(err == nil); for each table in ["users","api_keys","pats","orgs","org_members","admin_config"]: var name string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?", table).Scan(&name); assert(name == table); db.Close()

test_cases.description: Schema creation is idempotent: calling Open on an existing database that already has all tables succeeds without error and does not modify existing data.
preconditions: A SQLite database file already initialized by a prior Open call
test_cases.expected: Second Open call returns nil error and a valid *DB handle; existing table contents are unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "i.db"); db1, err1 := Open(path); assert(err1 == nil); db1.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO admin_config VALUES ('key1','val1')`); db1.Close(); db2, err2 := Open(path); assert(err2 == nil); var v string; db2.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key='key1'").Scan(&v); assert(v == "val1"); db2.Close()

test_cases.description: The db package exports a DB struct with a SqlDB *sql.DB field that callers use to execute their own queries; no per-table query methods are defined on DB.
preconditions: internal/db package is compiled
test_cases.expected: DB struct has field SqlDB of type *sql.DB; no methods named after tables (e.g. GetUser, InsertApiKey) exist on DB
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); assert(db.SqlDB != nil); // static: DB type has no InsertUser, GetUser, etc. methods

test_cases.description: Open validates path, creates parent directory (mode 0700) if needed, opens the SQLite file, calls initDB, and returns a fully initialized *DB handle or an error with no partial states.
preconditions: A fresh path in a temp directory whose parent does not yet exist
test_cases.expected: Open returns (*DB, nil); DB handle is immediately usable for queries; on any failure, returns (nil, non-nil error)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "newdir", "apikit.db"); db, err := Open(path); assert(err == nil); assert(db != nil); assert(db.SqlDB != nil); db.Close()

test_cases.description: OpenMemory opens an in-memory SQLite database with full initialization and returns an independent isolated DB instance on each call.
preconditions: No filesystem state required
test_cases.expected: OpenMemory returns (*DB, nil); all six tables exist; each call produces an independent database with no shared state
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db1, err1 := OpenMemory(); assert(err1 == nil); db2, err2 := OpenMemory(); assert(err2 == nil); db1.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO admin_config VALUES ('k','v')`); var v string; db2.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key='k'").Scan(&v); assert(v == ""); db1.Close(); db2.Close()

test_cases.description: DB.Close closes the underlying *sql.DB and returns any error from that close operation.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory or Open
test_cases.expected: Close returns nil on a healthy open database; subsequent queries on db.SqlDB return an error after Close
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); err := db.Close(); assert(err == nil); _, queryErr := db.SqlDB.Exec("SELECT 1"); assert(queryErr != nil)

test_cases.description: DB.Ping calls db.SqlDB.PingContext(ctx) and returns nil when the connection is alive.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: Ping returns nil for a healthy database; no additional checks beyond connection liveness are performed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); err := db.Ping(context.Background()); assert(err == nil)

test_cases.description: WithTx begins a DEFERRED transaction, calls fn with the transaction handle, commits if fn returns nil, and returns nil to the caller.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory with the admin_config table available

input.fn: inserts a row and returns nil

test_cases.expected: WithTx returns nil; the inserted row is visible in subsequent queries outside the transaction
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); err := db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { _, e := tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO admin_config VALUES ('txkey','txval')`); return e }); assert(err == nil); var v string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key='txkey'").Scan(&v); assert(v == "txval")

test_cases.description: WithTx does not wrap or transform errors returned by fn; the exact error from fn is returned unchanged to the caller.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: WithTx returns exactly the same error value as returned by fn; errors.Is(err, sentinelErr) is true
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); sentinelErr := errors.New("test error"); err := db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { return sentinelErr }); assert(errors.Is(err, sentinelErr))

test_cases.description: WithTx silently discards rollback errors and returns the original error from fn when both fn and rollback fail.
preconditions: An open DB instance; fn returns an error; rollback may also fail (e.g. connection closed)
test_cases.expected: WithTx returns the original error from fn, not a rollback error; rollback error is silently discarded
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); originalErr := errors.New("original"); err := db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { return originalErr }); assert(errors.Is(err, originalErr))

test_cases.description: WithTx always uses SQLite's default DEFERRED transaction isolation; no IMMEDIATE or EXCLUSIVE transaction modes are used.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: WithTx succeeds with DEFERRED isolation; IMMEDIATE and EXCLUSIVE modes are not exposed by this API
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); err := db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { return nil }); assert(err == nil) // DEFERRED is verified by BeginTx(ctx, nil)

test_cases.description: WithTx forwards ctx to db.SqlDB.BeginTx; callers are responsible for passing ctx inside fn; WithTx does not install additional timeouts or deadlines.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: WithTx propagates the context to BeginTx; context cancellation inside fn causes rollback and returns the context error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()); cancel(); err := db.WithTx(ctx, func(tx) error { return ctx.Err() }); assert(errors.Is(err, context.Canceled))

test_cases.description: WrapError maps SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE to ErrConflict, SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY to ErrConflict, SQLITE_BUSY to ErrDatabaseLocked, SQLITE_LOCKED to ErrDatabaseLocked; returns nil for nil input; returns err unchanged for unknown codes.
preconditions: internal/db package is compiled; synthetic SQLite errors can be constructed
test_cases.expected: WrapError returns ErrConflict for constraint errors; ErrDatabaseLocked for busy/locked; nil for nil; original error for unknown
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert(WrapError(nil) == nil); assert(errors.Is(WrapError(syntheticUniqueErr), ErrConflict)); assert(errors.Is(WrapError(syntheticBusyErr), ErrDatabaseLocked)); genericErr := errors.New("other"); assert(errors.Is(WrapError(genericErr), genericErr))

test_cases.description: WrapError is a pure function that performs no I/O or state modification and can be called with synthetically constructed errors in tests without a live database.
preconditions: No database connection required; synthetic SQLite error values are constructed in-process

input.err: synthetically constructed SQLite error with SQLITE_BUSY code

test_cases.expected: WrapError returns ErrDatabaseLocked without opening any connection or causing any side effects
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: syntheticBusy := constructSQLiteError(SQLITE_BUSY); result := WrapError(syntheticBusy); assert(errors.Is(result, ErrDatabaseLocked)); // no database connection used

test_cases.description: WrapError does not produce ErrNotFound; callers must return ErrNotFound explicitly when sql.ErrNoRows is encountered.
preconditions: No database connection required
test_cases.expected: WrapError returns sql.ErrNoRows unchanged (not ErrNotFound); callers must handle sql.ErrNoRows separately
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result := WrapError(sql.ErrNoRows); assert(!errors.Is(result, ErrNotFound)); assert(errors.Is(result, sql.ErrNoRows))

test_cases.description: The db package exports three sentinel errors: ErrNotFound, ErrConflict, and ErrDatabaseLocked, all testable via errors.Is.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
test_cases.expected: ErrNotFound.Error() == "db: not found"; ErrConflict.Error() == "db: conflict"; ErrDatabaseLocked.Error() == "db: database locked"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert(ErrNotFound.Error() == "db: not found"); assert(ErrConflict.Error() == "db: conflict"); assert(ErrDatabaseLocked.Error() == "db: database locked")

test_cases.description: The db package exports a TimeFormat constant equal to '2006-01-02T15:04:05Z'.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert(TimeFormat == "2006-01-02T15:04:05Z")

test_cases.description: FormatTime truncates to whole-second precision, converts to UTC, and formats using TimeFormat; sub-second components are discarded.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
test_cases.expected: FormatTime returns "2026-07-17T09:00:00Z" (converted to UTC, truncated to second, formatted with Z suffix)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: t := time.Date(2026,7,17,14,30,0,999999999,time.FixedZone("IST",5*3600+30*60)); result := FormatTime(t); assert(result == "2026-07-17T09:00:00Z")

test_cases.description: ParseTime parses a canonical RFC 3339 UTC timestamp string and returns the time.Time in UTC; returns an error for invalid strings.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
test_cases.expected: ParseTime returns (time.Time{2026-07-17 14:30:00 UTC}, nil); ParseTime with invalid string returns (zero time, non-nil error)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: t, err := ParseTime("2026-07-17T14:30:00Z"); assert(err == nil); assert(t.Year() == 2026 && t.Month() == 7 && t.Day() == 17 && t.Hour() == 14 && t.Minute() == 30 && t.Second() == 0 && t.Location() == time.UTC); _, badErr := ParseTime("not-a-timestamp"); assert(badErr != nil)

test_cases.description: FormatTime and ParseTime guarantee round-trip consistency: ParseTime(FormatTime(t)) equals t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC() for any time.Time.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported

input.t: arbitrary time.Time with sub-second components and non-UTC timezone

test_cases.expected: ParseTime(FormatTime(t)) equals t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC()
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: t := time.Now().Add(time.Duration(12345678) * time.Nanosecond).In(time.FixedZone("X", 3600)); result, err := ParseTime(FormatTime(t)); assert(err == nil); assert(result.Equal(t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC()))

test_cases.description: initDB calls SetMaxOpenConns(1) on the *sql.DB handle to enforce a single active connection, preventing concurrent-writer errors.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via Open
test_cases.expected: db.SqlDB.Stats().MaxOpenConnections == 1 after Open returns
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "j.db")); assert(err == nil); assert(db.SqlDB.Stats().MaxOpenConnections == 1); db.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB calls SetMaxIdleConns(1) on the *sql.DB handle to keep the single connection warm and avoid open/close overhead.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: After OpenMemory, the pool configuration has max idle connections = 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := OpenMemory(); assert(err == nil); // Go sql.DB.Stats() does not directly expose MaxIdleConns; verify indirectly by confirming pool config was applied via code inspection or Stats().Idle == 1 after one query; db.Close()

test_cases.description: OpenMemory relies on SetMaxOpenConns(1) to guarantee each :memory: DSN maps to exactly one connection (one isolated in-memory database instance) with no shared state between calls.
preconditions: No filesystem state required
test_cases.expected: Two OpenMemory calls produce fully isolated database instances; data written to one is never visible in the other
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db1, _ := OpenMemory(); db2, _ := OpenMemory(); db1.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO admin_config VALUES ('isolated','yes')`); var v string; db2.SqlDB.QueryRow(`SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key='isolated'`).Scan(&v); assert(v == ""); db1.Close(); db2.Close()

test_cases.description: initDB executes PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON via db.Exec so that SQLite enforces foreign key constraints after Open.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: INSERT into api_keys with a non-existent user_id returns a foreign key constraint error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); _, err := db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO api_keys VALUES ('k1','no-such-user','hash',30,NULL,NULL,'2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); assert(err != nil)

test_cases.description: The org_members table enforces ON DELETE CASCADE on org_id so membership rows are automatically removed when the referenced org is deleted.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory with a user, an org, and an org_member row inserted
test_cases.expected: After deleting org1, the corresponding row in org_members is automatically deleted; no orphaned membership rows remain
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO users VALUES ('u1','user1','e@e.com',NULL,'user','active','gh','gh1','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO orgs VALUES ('org1','OrgName','org-slug',NULL,'active','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO org_members VALUES ('org1','u1','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); db.SqlDB.Exec(`DELETE FROM orgs WHERE id='org1'`); var count int; db.SqlDB.QueryRow(`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM org_members WHERE org_id='org1'`).Scan(&count); assert(count == 0)

test_cases.description: The integration test suite uses a real SQLite file in a temp directory from t.TempDir() for file-based tests and OpenMemory for in-memory tests.
preconditions: Test binary is compiled with internal/db; Go test runner is available
test_cases.expected: Running 'go test ./internal/db/...' passes all integration tests; no real files are left behind after tests complete
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: // Verified by running: go test ./internal/db/... -v; assert(exit code == 0)

test_cases.description: TestOpen_EmptyPath verifies that Open("") returns a descriptive error without creating any filesystem state.
preconditions: No filesystem side effects from the empty-path test
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error with message 'db: path must not be empty'; no directories or files are created
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(""); assert(db == nil); assert(err.Error() == "db: path must not be empty")

test_cases.description: TestOpen_PathIsDirectory verifies that Open returns a descriptive error when given a path pointing to an existing directory.
preconditions: An existing directory created via t.TempDir()
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error containing the path and 'is a directory, not a file'; *DB is nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: dir := t.TempDir(); db, err := Open(dir); assert(db == nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), dir)); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), "is a directory, not a file"))

test_cases.description: TestOpen_CreatesDirectory verifies Open creates a missing parent directory and on non-Windows asserts mode 0700.
preconditions: Temp base directory from t.TempDir() exists; subdirectory of the database path does not exist
test_cases.expected: Directory <tempdir>/subdir is created by Open; on non-Windows, its permission mode is 0700
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: base := t.TempDir(); path := filepath.Join(base, "subdir", "apikit.db"); db, err := Open(path); assert(err == nil); assert(directoryExists(filepath.Dir(path))); if runtime.GOOS != "windows" { info, _ := os.Stat(filepath.Dir(path)); assert(info.Mode().Perm() == 0700) }; db.Close()

test_cases.description: TestOpen_CreatesSchema verifies all six tables exist after Open on a new database.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path in a temp directory
test_cases.expected: All six tables (users, api_keys, pats, orgs, org_members, admin_config) exist in sqlite_master after Open
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "schema.db")); assert(err == nil); for each table in ["users","api_keys","pats","orgs","org_members","admin_config"]: var n string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name=?", table).Scan(&n); assert(n == table); db.Close()

test_cases.description: TestOpen_Idempotent verifies that calling Open twice on the same path succeeds without error.
preconditions: A database file already initialized by a first Open call
test_cases.expected: Second Open call returns (non-nil *DB, nil error); schema is unchanged
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "idem.db"); db1, err1 := Open(path); assert(err1 == nil); db1.Close(); db2, err2 := Open(path); assert(err2 == nil); assert(db2 != nil); db2.Close()

test_cases.description: TestOpen_WALMode queries PRAGMA journal_mode and asserts the value is 'wal'; also verifies Open returns an error if WAL mode cannot be set.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database path on a WAL-supporting filesystem
test_cases.expected: PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'wal' after Open; WAL mode error path is covered by logic/unit inspection
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, err := Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "wal.db")); assert(err == nil); var mode string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow("PRAGMA journal_mode").Scan(&mode); assert(mode == "wal"); db.Close()

test_cases.description: TestOpen_CorruptFile verifies that Open returns a wrapped error containing the file path when the target file contains non-SQLite bytes.
preconditions: A file containing arbitrary non-SQLite bytes at the target path
test_cases.expected: Open returns non-nil error containing the file path; errors.Unwrap returns the original driver error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "corrupt.db"); os.WriteFile(path, []byte("this is not sqlite"), 0644); db, err := Open(path); assert(db == nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), path)); assert(errors.Unwrap(err) != nil)

test_cases.description: TestOpen_ForeignKeys verifies that a foreign key violation is rejected when inserting a child row with a non-existent parent.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory with foreign keys enabled

input.insert: api_keys row with user_id that does not exist in users

test_cases.expected: INSERT returns a non-nil error (SQLite foreign key constraint violation)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); _, err := db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO api_keys VALUES ('key1','nonexistent-user','hash',30,NULL,NULL,'2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); assert(err != nil)

test_cases.description: TestPing verifies that Ping returns nil on a healthy database connection.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
test_cases.expected: Ping returns nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); err := db.Ping(context.Background()); assert(err == nil)

test_cases.description: TestWithTx_Commit verifies that changes made inside WithTx are visible after the function returns nil.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory

input.fn: inserts a row into admin_config and returns nil

test_cases.expected: Row inserted inside WithTx is visible via subsequent query on db.SqlDB after commit
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO admin_config VALUES ('c','v')`); return nil }); var v string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow(`SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key='c'`).Scan(&v); assert(v == "v")

test_cases.description: TestWithTx_Rollback verifies that changes made inside WithTx are not visible after the function returns an error.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory

input.fn: inserts a row into admin_config and returns an error

test_cases.expected: Row inserted inside WithTx is NOT visible in subsequent query; WithTx returns the error from fn
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); retErr := errors.New("fail"); err := db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { tx.ExecContext(ctx, `INSERT INTO admin_config VALUES ('r','v')`); return retErr }); assert(errors.Is(err, retErr)); var v string; db.SqlDB.QueryRow(`SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key='r'`).Scan(&v); assert(v == "")

test_cases.description: TestWrapError_Conflict verifies that a UNIQUE constraint violation produced by a real INSERT maps to ErrConflict via WrapError.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory with a user row already inserted
test_cases.expected: WrapError maps the returned driver error to ErrConflict; errors.Is(wrappedErr, ErrConflict) == true
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO users VALUES ('id1','u1','e@e.com',NULL,'user','active','gh','gh1','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); _, err := db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO users VALUES ('id2','u1','e2@e.com',NULL,'user','active','gh','gh2','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); wrapped := WrapError(err); assert(errors.Is(wrapped, ErrConflict))

test_cases.description: TestWrapError_Locked constructs a synthetic SQLite error with SQLITE_BUSY error code and passes it to WrapError, verifying ErrDatabaseLocked is returned without a live database.
preconditions: internal/db and modernc.org/sqlite packages are available; no live database connection is required

input.err: synthetically constructed SQLite error with SQLITE_BUSY code

test_cases.expected: WrapError returns ErrDatabaseLocked; errors.Is(result, ErrDatabaseLocked) == true; no database connection is needed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: syntheticBusy := constructSQLiteError(SQLITE_BUSY_CODE); result := WrapError(syntheticBusy); assert(errors.Is(result, ErrDatabaseLocked))

test_cases.description: TestWrapError_Passthrough verifies that an unknown error is returned unchanged by WrapError.
preconditions: No database connection required
test_cases.expected: WrapError returns the same error value unchanged; errors.Is(result, originalErr) == true
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: original := errors.New("unknown"); result := WrapError(original); assert(errors.Is(result, original))

test_cases.description: TestFormatTime verifies truncation to whole-second precision, UTC normalization, and correct TimeFormat output string.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported

input.t: time with sub-second nanos and non-UTC zone

test_cases.expected: FormatTime returns a string matching 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ' in UTC with no sub-second components
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: t := time.Date(2026,1,15,10,30,45,123456789, time.FixedZone("EST",-5*3600)); result := FormatTime(t); assert(result == "2026-01-15T15:30:45Z"); assert(!strings.Contains(result, "."))

test_cases.description: TestParseTime verifies round-trip consistency: ParseTime(FormatTime(t)) equals t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC().
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
test_cases.expected: ParseTime(FormatTime(t)) equals t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC()
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: t := time.Now(); formatted := FormatTime(t); parsed, err := ParseTime(formatted); assert(err == nil); assert(parsed.Equal(t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC()))

property_tests.description: For any valid file path, both Open and OpenMemory produce DB handles with identical schema (all six tables present) and identical configuration (pool=1, foreign keys ON), confirming they share initDB.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate valid file paths via t.TempDir()+filepath.Join; pair each with an OpenMemory call
property_tests.invariant_check: verifyTablesExist(db.SqlDB) returns true for all six tables; PRAGMA foreign_keys returns 1; Stats().MaxOpenConnections == 1 for both db1 (from Open) and db2 (from OpenMemory)

property_tests.description: For any number of Open calls on the same existing database file, Open always succeeds and the existing table contents are never modified.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Repeat Open on the same path N times (N in [2..5]); after first Open, insert a sentinel row in admin_config
property_tests.invariant_check: Each subsequent Open returns nil error and non-nil *DB; querying admin_config for the sentinel row returns the original value unchanged

property_tests.description: For any time.Time value t (including sub-second precision, non-UTC zones, past and future dates), ParseTime(FormatTime(t)) equals t.Truncate(time.Second).UTC().
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary time.Time values using random Unix timestamps in range [0, 4102444800] with random nanosecond offsets and random timezone offsets

property_tests.description: For any error value passed to WrapError (nil, known SQLite codes, unknown codes, wrapped chains), the output is deterministic and no I/O or state changes occur.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate error inputs: nil; synthetic SQLite errors for UNIQUE, PK, BUSY, LOCKED; arbitrary errors; errors wrapped via fmt.Errorf
property_tests.invariant_check: WrapError called twice with the same input returns the same output both times; calling WrapError does not modify any global state; no goroutines are spawned

property_tests.description: For any WithTx call where fn returns a non-nil error, no writes performed inside fn are visible in the database after WithTx returns.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary INSERT statements to admin_config; fn performs the INSERT then returns a non-nil error
property_tests.invariant_check: After WithTx returns non-nil error: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM admin_config equals the count before the WithTx call; the transaction was fully rolled back

property_tests.description: For any call to Open or OpenMemory, the result is always either (*DB with all initialization complete, nil) or (nil, non-nil error) — no partially initialized DB handle is ever returned.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Call Open with: valid paths, empty paths, directory paths, corrupt file paths; call OpenMemory with no arguments
property_tests.invariant_check: If err == nil then db != nil AND db.SqlDB != nil AND PRAGMA foreign_keys returns 1 AND all six tables exist; if err != nil then db == nil

property_tests.description: For any two distinct OpenMemory calls, writes to one instance are never visible in the other; each call returns a fully independent isolated database.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Call OpenMemory N times (N in [2..5]); perform arbitrary INSERT operations on each; cross-check all others for absence of those rows
property_tests.invariant_check: For each pair (db_i, db_j) where i != j: rows inserted into db_i.SqlDB are not visible when queried from db_j.SqlDB

property_tests.description: For any call to Open with an empty path or a path pointing to an existing directory, Open returns the descriptive error without creating directories, opening database connections, or producing any filesystem side effects.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Call Open with empty string; call Open with paths that are existing directories (from t.TempDir())
property_tests.invariant_check: Open returns non-nil error and nil *DB; no new filesystem entries are created; the directory listing of the test temp dir is unchanged after the call

edge_case_tests.description: Open with a path containing a trailing slash that resolves to an existing directory returns the descriptive directory error; no database connection is opened.
preconditions: A directory exists at the target path
edge_case_tests.expected: Open returns non-nil error containing the path and 'is a directory, not a file'; *DB is nil
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: dir := t.TempDir(); path := dir + string(os.PathSeparator); db, err := Open(path); assert(db == nil); assert(err != nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), "is a directory, not a file"))

edge_case_tests.description: Open returns the OS error from directory creation without opening any database connection when the parent directory cannot be created due to a permission error.
preconditions: A path where the parent directory is unwritable (e.g. root-owned directory without write permission); test runs as non-root on Linux
edge_case_tests.expected: Open returns non-nil OS error; *DB is nil; no partial directory is created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: if runtime.GOOS == "windows" || os.Getuid() == 0 { t.Skip() }; db, err := Open("/root/no-permission/apikit.db"); assert(db == nil); assert(err != nil)

edge_case_tests.description: When the schema creation transaction fails partway through, initDB rolls back and returns an error; no partially-created tables persist.
preconditions: A mechanism to cause schema creation failure (e.g. disk-full simulation or pre-corrupted DB state); this is largely verified by idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS behavior
edge_case_tests.expected: initDB returns a non-nil error; calling constructor returns nil *DB; no partial table set is visible in sqlite_master
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: // This edge case is verified indirectly: Open on an already-locked or read-only path returns error and nil *DB; assert no partial tables exist; db, err := Open(readOnlyPath); assert(err != nil); assert(db == nil)

edge_case_tests.description: When the PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON execution fails with a driver error, initDB returns that error immediately without attempting schema creation.
preconditions: A database connection where PRAGMA execution fails (e.g. read-only database)

input.action: PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON on a connection that rejects it

edge_case_tests.expected: initDB returns non-nil error; calling constructor returns nil *DB; no schema creation is attempted
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: // Verified via corrupt file test: if driver fails on any PRAGMA, Open returns wrapped error and nil *DB; db, err := Open(corruptPath); assert(err != nil); assert(db == nil)

edge_case_tests.description: When PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL query fails with a driver error on a corrupt file, Open wraps the error with the file path and returns it.
preconditions: A file at the target path containing arbitrary non-SQLite bytes
edge_case_tests.expected: Open returns non-nil error wrapped with 'db: failed to open database at "<path>"'; errors.Unwrap returns original driver error; *DB is nil
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "corrupt.db"); os.WriteFile(path, []byte("garbage bytes"), 0644); db, err := Open(path); assert(db == nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), "failed to open database at")); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), path)); assert(errors.Unwrap(err) != nil)

edge_case_tests.description: When a file at path contains arbitrary non-SQLite bytes, the driver returns an error on first PRAGMA; Open wraps it with the file path and closes the *sql.DB handle before returning.
preconditions: A file containing random non-SQLite bytes at the target path
edge_case_tests.expected: Open returns non-nil error containing both 'failed to open database at' and the file path; original driver error is accessible via errors.Unwrap; *DB is nil
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "random.db"); os.WriteFile(path, []byte{0xFF, 0xFE, 0xAA, 0xBB}, 0644); db, err := Open(path); assert(db == nil); assert(strings.Contains(err.Error(), path)); unwrapped := errors.Unwrap(err); assert(unwrapped != nil)

edge_case_tests.description: When the table creation transaction is interrupted before COMMIT, SQLite rolls it back automatically; subsequent Open creates the schema from scratch with no partial state.
preconditions: A database file that was partially written (simulated by closing connection before COMMIT)
edge_case_tests.expected: A subsequent Open call on the same path succeeds; all six tables exist; no partial schema state persists
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: path := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "interrupted.db"); // First open creates file but simulates interrupted tx; db1, _ := Open(path); db1.Close(); db2, err2 := Open(path); assert(err2 == nil); for each table in ["users","api_keys","pats","orgs","org_members","admin_config"]: assert(tableExists(db2.SqlDB, table)); db2.Close()

edge_case_tests.description: DB.Ping with a pre-cancelled context returns the context error immediately; the readiness probe would return HTTP 503.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
edge_case_tests.expected: Ping returns non-nil context cancellation error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()); cancel(); err := db.Ping(ctx); assert(err != nil); assert(errors.Is(err, context.Canceled))

edge_case_tests.description: The db package never calls os.Exit or panic in response to any error condition; all errors are returned via return values.
preconditions: internal/db source code is available for inspection
edge_case_tests.expected: No calls to os.Exit, panic (other than panic("not implemented")), or log.Fatal exist in the db package source files
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: // Static verification: grep -r 'os.Exit\|log.Fatal\|panic(' internal/db/*.go; assert(no results for os.Exit or log.Fatal); assert(no uncontrolled panics in error paths)

edge_case_tests.description: When fn returns a context cancellation error, WithTx rolls back the transaction (discarding rollback error) and returns the context error.
preconditions: An open DB instance returned by OpenMemory
edge_case_tests.expected: WithTx returns context.Canceled error; no changes from fn are visible in the database
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background()); cancel(); err := db.WithTx(ctx, func(tx) error { return context.Canceled }); assert(errors.Is(err, context.Canceled))

edge_case_tests.description: When BeginTx fails (e.g. database connection is closed), WithTx returns the BeginTx error immediately without calling fn.
preconditions: A DB instance whose underlying *sql.DB has been closed
edge_case_tests.expected: WithTx returns non-nil error from BeginTx; fn is never invoked
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); db.Close(); fnCalled := false; err := db.WithTx(context.Background(), func(tx) error { fnCalled = true; return nil }); assert(err != nil); assert(fnCalled == false)

edge_case_tests.description: WrapError correctly maps a SQLite error code nested inside a wrapped error chain (e.g. via fmt.Errorf("%w")) to the appropriate sentinel.
preconditions: No database connection required; a synthetic SQLite error wrapped via fmt.Errorf
edge_case_tests.expected: WrapError returns ErrConflict even when the SQLite error is nested inside the error chain
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: inner := constructSQLiteError(SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_UNIQUE_CODE); wrapped := fmt.Errorf("outer: %w", inner); result := WrapError(wrapped); assert(errors.Is(result, ErrConflict))

edge_case_tests.description: FormatTime converts a time with a non-UTC timezone offset to UTC before formatting; the output always ends with 'Z' and never contains a numeric timezone offset.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
edge_case_tests.expected: FormatTime returns "2026-07-17T09:00:00Z" (converted from IST to UTC); no "+05:30" in output

edge_case_tests.description: ParseTime returns a parse error when the input string has a numeric timezone offset (e.g. '+05:30') instead of the 'Z' suffix.
preconditions: internal/db package is imported
edge_case_tests.expected: ParseTime returns non-nil error; the returned time.Time is the zero value
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: t, err := ParseTime("2026-07-17T14:30:00+05:30"); assert(err != nil); assert(t.IsZero())

edge_case_tests.description: Inserting an api_keys row with a non-existent user_id causes SQLite to return a SQLITE_CONSTRAINT error; WrapError maps it to ErrConflict.
preconditions: A fresh SQLite database opened via OpenMemory with foreign keys enabled
edge_case_tests.expected: INSERT returns non-nil driver error; WrapError maps it to ErrConflict; errors.Is(wrapped, ErrConflict) == true
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db, _ := OpenMemory(); defer db.Close(); _, err := db.SqlDB.Exec(`INSERT INTO api_keys VALUES ('k1','nonexistent','hash',30,NULL,NULL,'2026-01-01T00:00:00Z')`); assert(err != nil); wrapped := WrapError(err); assert(errors.Is(wrapped, ErrConflict))

smoke_tests.description: Full happy-path smoke test: Open creates a missing parent directory, initializes WAL mode and foreign keys, creates all six tables, and returns a usable *DB handle.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call db.Open(filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "newdir", "apikit.db")) from a test function
mockable: os.MkdirAll (can be verified via filesystem state)
expected_effects: Parent directory <tempdir>/newdir is created with mode 0700 (on non-Windows)
SQLite database file is created at <tempdir>/newdir/apikit.db
All six tables (users, api_keys, pats, orgs, org_members, admin_config) exist in sqlite_master

smoke_tests.description: Full happy-path smoke test for OpenMemory: returns an independent in-memory DB with full initialization (no WAL, FK ON, all six tables).
smoke_tests.trigger: Call db.OpenMemory() from a downstream test function
expected_effects: All six tables exist in sqlite_master
A second OpenMemory call produces an independent isolated database instance

smoke_tests.description: Error-path smoke test: Open on a corrupt file (non-SQLite bytes) returns a wrapped error containing the file path; no *DB handle is returned.
smoke_tests.trigger: Write arbitrary bytes to a temp file, then call db.Open(path) on that file
expected_effects: Error message contains 'db: failed to open database at' and the file path
errors.Unwrap(err) returns the original driver error
No *DB handle is leaked; underlying *sql.DB is closed

smoke_tests.description: Full happy-path smoke test for WithTx commit: changes made by fn inside a transaction are durably visible after commit.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call db.WithTx(ctx, fn) where fn inserts a row into admin_config and returns nil
expected_effects: The row inserted inside fn is visible via subsequent db.SqlDB.QueryRow call

smoke_tests.description: Error-path smoke test for WithTx rollback: changes made by fn are not visible after fn returns an error; ErrConflict is propagated.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call db.WithTx(ctx, fn) where fn inserts a row and returns db.WrapError of a UNIQUE constraint violation
expected_effects: No rows from the failed fn are visible in the database after rollback
Rollback errors (if any) are silently discarded
